summary: Multiple suspicious install-time and exfiltration-shaped patterns require human review
details: Package ships a postinstall agent (scripts/postinstall-agent.mjs) and several runtime modules (dist/relayServer.js, dist/discordRelayUpload.js, dist/secretScan/*) containing patterns that pattern-match exfiltration shapes: base64-decoded blobs in deploymentDefaults.js and hfCredentials.js, network-command keywords (ping, POST) co-located with HTTP calls in discordRelayUpload.js and relayServer.js, hardcoded fetch() calls to huggingface.co in secretScan/agentStartupAudit.js, and network/identifier keywords inside the postinstall script. The keyword co-occurrences are concerning given the postinstall trigger and a Discord-named relay module, but semantic tracing of the actual code paths was not completed, so it is not confirmed whether the postinstall script actually performs outbound network or merely declares constants, whether the Discord/HF endpoints are user-configured destinations or hardcoded attacker C2, and whether the base64 blobs decode to code (eval/exec) or to configuration data. Without that confirmation, the evidence does not meet the threshold for a published block verdict. Routing to human review for code-level confirmation of postinstall behavior and the destination/usage of the base64 payloads.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
